Name: Acy
Text: Cool to see someone with these devices "out in the wild" since I usually hear about them from youtubers and blogs who specialize in them. You custom pixel decorations are so cute! I have an RG280M, and it's not perfect and I think it's older so the CFW options are more limited, but it's a really neat device. I might get a newer one some time, but the metal purple/pink gradient is so pretty that nothing else feels like a real "upgrade" even if they're actually more powerful lol. (This comment originally had an image attached - it is unavailable due to me changing comment hosting)

Name: Stupied
Text: Nekoweb's content security policy is a lot more relaxed than Neocities so it works there!

Name: Stupied
Text: As for the neocities free users thing, it does not affect new users who made their accounts before the change (i forgor the range but probably around july-ish of 2024?). Also if you pay for supporter once and stop paying it before the month ends, you will still have CORs support and a less strict content security policy ^_^

Name: Stupied
Text:
# HENLOOO THERE
Some markdown tests for future reference. Also the comment below is what it looks like when it hasn't been approved!!!!!!

## This is a Heading h2
### This is a Heading h3
#### This is a Heading h4
##### This is a Heading h5
###### This is a Heading h6

## Emphasis

*This text will be italic*  
_This will also be italic_

**This text will be bold**  
__This will also be bold__

_You **can** combine them_

## Lists

### Unordered

* Item 1
* Item 2
* Item 2a
* Item 2b
    * Item 3a
    * Item 3b

### Ordered

1. Item 1
2. Item 2
3. Item 3
    1. Item 3a
    2. Item 3b

## Images

![This is an alt text.](/image/Markdown-mark.svg "This is a sample image.")

## Links

You may be using [Markdown Live Preview](https://markdownlivepreview.com/).

## Blockquotes

> Markdown is a lightweight markup language with plain-text-formatting syntax, created in 2004 by John Gruber with Aaron Swartz.
>
>> Markdown is often used to format readme files, for writing messages in online discussion forums, and to create rich text using a plain text editor.

## Tables

| Left columns  | Right columns |
| ------------- |:-------------:|
| left foo      | right foo     |
| left bar      | right bar     |
| left baz      | right baz     |

## Blocks of code

```
let message = 'Hello world';
alert(message);
```

## Inline code
Welcome to `stupied.net`

Name: Dellus
Text: I know this is a strange question but are we allowed to send you stuff and have you judge it?

Name: Stupied
Text: Suppose you could, but frankly not sure if my opinions would be much of assistance. What would you even want me to judge..?

Name: Stupied
Text: Thank you! My website(s) houses literally everything I do so it's kinda all over the place x3

Name: Dellus
Text: In short the usability of ideas.Does a specific action make a character too unlikable?Is a nonlinear story too confusing or should it only be revealed when the protagonist learns the truth?When should you rely on tropes and when to subvert them?

Name: Stupied
Text: That sounds fun and you definitely could! But really wouldn't trust the advice of someone who calls herself "Stupied" ahaha

Name: kestrel
Text: Your website is SO PRETTY. Everything about it, from the starting menu to the backgrounds. Are we allowed to take inspiration from your page for our own?

Name: Stupied
Text: To put it bluntly, I do not care what others do with my work. We've all at one point been inspired by something and, whether intentionally or not,  model ourselves accordingly to it. Originality is relative. So please, feel free to nab ideas from my website. Credit absolutely not required! It's not like I invented putting images in grids,,,
such an lovely website. everything is so lively (mostly attributed to the animations. so smooth) and makes me realize going simple is a good way to go in terms of webdesign. keep it up

Text: Your website is so cool OMGG!! I love the way everything's so organized, and don't even get me started on the way the little animations. If you don't mind me asking, where/how did you exactly learn how to code this well? I'm trying to make a website of my own, but I'm somewhat struggling ;-;

Name: sam
Text: I LOVE YOUR WEBSITE, i see your work and it´s AMAZING, i love all the texture and stuff, you have a pack of brushes?? also how long did it take you to do all of this? have you been codding for many years?

Name: Stupied
Text: Thank you, started this neocities website 2-ish years ago with very little coding experience. If an idiot like me can figure it out, then just about anyone can! As for the brushes, mostly use the default brushes in krita and the ones made by this person » https://tombofnull.itch.io/

Name: Stupied
Text: Through the scientific method of fucking around and finding out. There isn't really a singular definitive resource to learn, nor is there a shortcut to it. Instead you just search it and snoop through other's code. Sorry, the struggle is par for the course and you just have learn to persevere through it.

Name: Stupied
Text: Aw, that's neat. Bestest of luck! Also, thank you for answering the 100 Artist Questionnaire, it was fun to read. Though I'll have to politely disagree about the formal education stagnating creativity. Those environments don't do anything but provide you fundamentals (and connections, but that's not the point rn). Whether you take those guidelines to heart is completely your choice. You can learn what the teach but do something completely different. Also art doesn't need to be vulnerable to be good. It can be mischievous and cryptic! It can whimsical and nonsensical. Good art doesn't need to super personal to be appealing. Besides, I've seen a handful of 'vulnerable' art that feels more navel-gazing, the annoying kind, rather than genuine.

Name: Samuel
Text: Your site is so beautiful omg, te color scheme is amazing and everything is very nicely organized but my favorite part is the art style, I love your art and I wish I had money to commision because it's truly fantastic!

Name: chunkofwax
Text: thanks! I prob could have elaborated or better explained my position lol, but I get what you mean. I think vulnerable is probably the wrong choice of word for what I was trying to get at, but it's whatever. I agree with you on the most part, I don't want to suggest that you can't learn anything of value in art school, or find success in that environment. I just personally dislike the notion that going through these institutions is the only way to learn, especially when there's all sorts of other variables that can really sour your experience. More so a critique of how these institutions are run in general, regardless of subject or major, rather than art school as a concept. Saying that all academic environments are BAD was the wrong kind of generalized, blanket statement on my part lmao. I was just writin something to fill in the blanks. Thank you for reading and writing though :)

Name: Axel
Text: Your art gallery page looks so good!!! any tips/tutorials/resources to give if someone wants to make something similar?

Name: Stupied
Text:
There's so many ways to go about making a gallery but I strongly suggest learning some javascript or a static site generator. There's a bit of a learning curve but it will save you so many headaches across the line. There's also a bunch of other scripts made by other people
- [Espy's Gallery Grid](https://espy.world/log/2026/feb/gallery-grid-release)
- [MelonKing's Gallery Maker](https://melonking.net/melon?z=/free/software/gallery-maker)
- [Metemo's Do Gallery](https://metemo.neocities.org/do_gallery/)

Also this [blog post](https://stupied.net/blog/2025-10-11-Art-Archive) vaguely going over how I went about making my art archive
